questions.question: Explain the three main components of a computer bus and state their functions.
keyPoints: Data bus carries actual data between components - bidirectional
Address bus carries memory addresses - unidirectional from CPU
Control bus carries control signals like READ, WRITE, MEMORY READ/WRITE

questions.question: What is bus arbitration? Describe any two methods of bus arbitration.
keyPoints: Bus arbitration resolves conflicts when multiple devices want to use the bus simultaneously
Daisy chain: priority based on physical position in chain, simple but not flexible
Independent request: separate request/grant lines for each device, more complex but flexible

questions.question: Differentiate between synchronous and asynchronous buses.
keyPoints: Synchronous buses use a common clock signal for timing, simpler to implement, faster
Asynchronous buses use handshaking signals (REQ/ACK), more flexible, can connect devices of different speeds

questions.question: What is bus mastering? Explain its significance in computer systems.
keyPoints: Bus mastering allows devices to take control of the bus and initiate transfers independently
Used by DMA controllers and intelligent I/O devices
Reduces CPU overhead and improves system performance for bulk data transfers

questions.question: Calculate the maximum addressable memory for a system with 20 address lines. Also, if the data bus is 8 bits, how many memory locations can be accessed in one read operation?
keyPoints: 20 address lines can address 2^20 = 1,048,576 locations (1 MB)
8-bit data bus reads 1 byte (1 memory location) per read operation
Total memory accessed in one operation = 1 byte

questions.question: What is the relationship between bus width, bus clock frequency, and bus bandwidth?
keyPoints: Bus bandwidth = Bus width × Clock frequency
Wider bus and higher frequency increase data transfer rate
Example: 32-bit bus at 100 MHz = 400 MB/s bandwidth

questions.question: Explain the purpose of wait states in bus operations.
keyPoints: Wait states are idle clock cycles inserted when slow devices cannot keep up with fast CPU
Allows memory or I/O devices additional time to complete operations
Prevents data loss or errors during data transfers

questions.question: With the help of a diagram, explain the process of a typical memory READ operation showing the role of address bus, data bus, and control bus.
keyPoints: CPU places memory address on address bus
CPU sends MEMORY READ signal on control bus
Memory places requested data on data bus
Memory sends READY signal to indicate data is available
CPU reads data from data bus and completes the operation
Timing diagram shows address valid, read signal, data valid sequence

questions.question: Compare daisy chain, polling, and independent request methods of bus arbitration with respect to their advantages, disadvantages, and suitable applications.
keyPoints: Daisy chain: simple, fixed priority, cascade failure, suitable for few devices
Polling: CPU intensive, fixed priority, simple hardware, suitable for embedded systems
Independent request: complex hardware, flexible priority, parallel processing, suitable for multiprocessor systems
Comparison table showing speed, cost, flexibility, scalability

questions.question: Explain the structure and functioning of the system bus in a computer. How does it differ from I/O buses and expansion buses?
keyPoints: System bus connects CPU, memory, and essential components
Consists of data, address, and control buses
Operates at highest frequency for maximum performance
I/O buses connect peripheral devices, operate at lower speeds
Expansion buses (PCI, USB) provide standardized connectivity for add-on devices
System bus is internal, I/O and expansion buses are external
